title: TMDB Movie VAD + Emotion Intensity Scores
subtitle: 1.38M movies with NRC valence, arousal, dominance and 8-emotion intensity scores from plot keywords
description:
## TMDB Movie Emotional Feature Matrix

All 24 original TMDB columns (~1.38M movies) enriched with affective scores derived from movie keywords using NRC lexicons by Saif M. Mohammad (National Research Council Canada).

### Scored Columns (12 added)

| Column | Source | Description |
|--------|--------|-------------|
| sentiment | derived | positive / negative / neutral / unknown |
| valence | NRC VAD | positivity of keyword associations (0–1) |
| arousal | NRC VAD | energy/activation level (0–1) |
| dominance | NRC VAD | sense of control/power (0–1) |
| anger … trust | NRC Intensity | mean emotion intensity across keywords (0–1) |

### Pipeline

```
TMDB keywords → tokenize on spaces/hyphens
             → lemmatize (NLTK, noun/verb/adj POS)
             → NRC lexicon lookup (first matching lemma)
             → average matched token scores per keyword
             → average keyword scores per movie
```

Source code: https://github.com/bdelanghe/imdb-kaggle

### Lexicon Sources

- NRC Emotion Lexicon (binary): https://saifmohammad.com/WebPages/NRC-Emotion-Lexicon.htm
- NRC Emotion Intensity Lexicon (continuous): https://saifmohammad.com/WebPages/AffectIntensity.htm
- NRC VAD Lexicon (valence/arousal/dominance): https://saifmohammad.com/WebPages/nrc-vad.html
- Full NRC lexicon index: https://saifmohammad.com/WebPages/lexicons.html

### Methodological Notes

Scores represent **perceived emotional word associations**, not ground-truth movie sentiment (Mohammad, 2020 — Practical and Ethical Considerations in the Effective use of Emotion and Sentiment Lexicons).

Key caveats:
- **Scores are relative** — valence 0.7 means "more positive than 0.6", not "70% positive"
- **Association ≠ denotation** — "party" associates with joy but does not mean joy
- **Dominant sense bias** — film-industry terms (shot, cut, cast) may carry non-cinematic scores
- **Coverage** — ~75% of rows have no TMDB keywords and are scored as unknown

Correct interpretation: *"Movies with these keywords contain more joy-associated language"* — not *"this movie is joyful."*

### Future Work / Related Lexicons

- Warriner VAD (13,915 lemmas) — strong human-rated validation source for NRC VAD
- ANEW — classic VAD norms (~1,000 words)
- LIWC — category-based psycholinguistic features (anger, social, power, death…)
- SenticNet — concept-level sentiment for multi-word phrases
- MovieLens Tag Genome — semantic movie tags with relevance scores

resources.description: All 24 original TMDB columns plus 12 emotion/VAD scores derived from movie keywords via NRC lexicons. One row per movie.

fields.name: backdrop_path
fields.description: Relative path to backdrop image on TMDB CDN

fields.name: poster_path
fields.description: Relative path to poster image on TMDB CDN

fields.name: keywords
fields.description: Comma-separated TMDB plot keywords (source for emotion scoring)

fields.name: sentiment
fields.description: Derived sentiment label: positive (valence>0.5), negative (valence<0.5), neutral, or unknown (no keywords)

fields.name: valence
fields.description: NRC VAD: mean keyword valence (0=negative, 1=positive). Relative scale — higher means more positive-associated keywords.

fields.name: anger
fields.description: NRC Intensity: mean anger intensity of keywords (0–1). 0 = no matched keywords.

fields.name: anticipation
fields.description: NRC Intensity: mean anticipation intensity of keywords (0–1)

fields.name: disgust
fields.description: NRC Intensity: mean disgust intensity of keywords (0–1)

fields.name: fear
fields.description: NRC Intensity: mean fear intensity of keywords (0–1)

fields.name: joy
fields.description: NRC Intensity: mean joy intensity of keywords (0–1)

fields.name: sadness
fields.description: NRC Intensity: mean sadness intensity of keywords (0–1)

fields.name: surprise
fields.description: NRC Intensity: mean surprise intensity of keywords (0–1)

fields.name: trust
fields.description: NRC Intensity: mean trust intensity of keywords (0–1)
